skill.name: seo-content-auditor
skill.description: Analyzes provided content for quality, E-E-A-T signals, readability, keyword use, structure, and SEO improvement opportunities.
skill.summary: Audits supplied content and recommends practical SEO, readability, structure, and trust-signal improvements.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and all detected patterns are false positives. The file contains instructional content only, with no executable shell commands, weak cryptographic use, network reconnaissance behavior, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Path Reference
low_findings.description: Line 25 uses backticks to format a relative documentation path. It does not invoke Ruby, shell execution, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a Markdown-formatted file path inside an instruction sentence. No command syntax, executable script, or user-controlled command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Line 3 is YAML description text about SEO and E-E-A-T analysis. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive code appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata text and contains no code. The apparent match is semantic noise from normal English words in the description.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Line 4 continues the YAML description with content review guidance. It does not reference or use a cryptographic primitive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is descriptive prose and contains no executable code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: Line 31 lists content depth as an SEO audit focus area. It does not contain network scanning, probing, or reconnaissance instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a content-audit checklist item. No network host, port, scanner command, or discovery workflow is present.

content.value_statement: Content teams need fast reviews that separate useful SEO improvements from vague advice. This skill scores supplied content and gives focused recommendations for quality, E-E-A-T, readability, keywords, structure, and trust signals.
actual_capabilities: Reviews supplied text for depth, originality, and coverage gaps.
Checks visible E-E-A-T signals, including expertise, citations, and trust indicators.
Assesses heading structure, formatting, readability, and user experience.
Reviews keyword density, distribution, and semantic relevance in provided content.
Produces a scored content audit with issues and prioritized recommendations.
Suggests missing topics, internal linking opportunities, and structure improvements.
limitations: Does not check live SERP rankings or search volume data.
Does not analyze competitor pages unless their content is provided.
Does not verify technical SEO metrics, crawl data, or page performance.
Does not validate real user engagement metrics or analytics data.

use_cases.title: Review Blog Drafts Before Publication
use_cases.description: Audit a draft for content depth, headings, keyword use, readability, and missing trust signals before publishing.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Landing Page Copy
use_cases.description: Find weak sections, unclear structure, missing proof points, and keyword gaps in provided landing page text.

use_cases.title: Standardize Editorial Quality Checks
use_cases.description: Apply a repeatable scorecard for E-E-A-T, readability, topic coverage, and actionable optimization tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Content Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one supplied article or page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this content for SEO quality, readability, structure, and E-E-A-T signals. Provide scores, issues, and clear improvement recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword and Structure Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review focused on target terms and page organization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this content for the target keyword: [keyword]. Assess keyword use, headings, semantic relevance, internal link opportunities, and missing sections.

prompt_templates.title: Editorial Quality Scorecard
prompt_templates.scenario: Team workflow for consistent pre-publication checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a content audit scorecard for this draft. Include category scores, evidence from the text, priority level, and specific edits for each issue.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Gap Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed audit using supplied audience, goal, and competing content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page using the audience, business goal, target keyword, and competitor excerpts provided. Identify content gaps, trust gaps, structure changes, and next-step revisions.

output_examples.input: A 1,200-word blog draft about cloud backup for small businesses.
output: Overall content quality score: 7 out of 10.
Strong points: clear audience fit, useful examples, and logical introduction.
Priority improvements: add source citations, compare backup methods, shorten long paragraphs, and include internal links to pricing and security pages.

output_examples.input: Landing page copy for an accounting software product.
output: Readability score: 8 out of 10; E-E-A-T score: 5 out of 10.
Main issue: the page explains features but does not show enough proof, credentials, or customer evidence.
Recommended changes: add customer outcomes, clarify who the product serves, and group feature sections under benefit-led headings.

output_examples.input: An older tutorial page targeting a competitive keyword.
output: Missing topics include prerequisites, common mistakes, examples, and a short comparison section.
Suggested structure: add a summary, improve heading hierarchy, and place related internal links after each major section.

best_practices: Provide the full content, target keyword, audience, and page goal for the most useful audit.
Ask for prioritized recommendations when time or publishing resources are limited.
Review suggestions against brand voice, compliance rules, and subject matter expert input.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to verify live rankings, analytics, or search volume without providing data.
Do not treat keyword density advice as a replacement for user intent and content usefulness.
Do not request competitor analysis unless competitor content or summaries are included.

faq.question: Can this skill audit live webpages?
faq.answer: It audits provided content. It does not fetch pages or verify live technical SEO data by itself.

faq.question: Does it replace a professional SEO tool?
faq.answer: No. It complements SEO tools by reviewing the quality, structure, and clarity of supplied content.

faq.question: Can it evaluate E-E-A-T?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks visible expertise, experience, authority, and trust signals in the provided text.

faq.question: Can it compare competitor content?
faq.answer: Yes, if you provide competitor excerpts or summaries. It cannot discover competitor pages on its own.

faq.question: What inputs improve the audit?
faq.answer: Include the draft, target keyword, intended audience, page goal, brand constraints, and any known SEO data.

faq.question: Will it write the final content?
faq.answer: It focuses on audit recommendations, but you can ask for revised sections based on the findings.
